messages.text: @channel P1 alert: EU region users getting SAML login failures from Okta. Error: 'SAML assertion is not yet valid'. Affects ~200 users in London and Frankfurt offices. Started approx 09:05 UTC.
Checking Okta admin console now. Seeing a spike in SAML assertion errors starting exactly 09:05. The error message is 'NotBefore condition not met'. This is a time/clock skew issue.
Confirmed. Our EU Okta tenant was recently migrated to a new data center cluster last night during the maintenance window. The NTP sync on the new nodes may have drifted.

Root cause confirmed: the new EU data center cluster has a clock skew of ~4 minutes compared to the Okta SP metadata clock tolerance which is set to 3 minutes by default. SAML assertions issued by the new nodes are timestamped 4 minutes ahead.
What's the fix? Can we update the clock skew tolerance in Okta?
Yes. In Okta Admin > Security > Identity Providers > [Our Okta SAML config] > Advanced Settings, changed 'Clock Skew' from 3 minutes to 7 minutes. This is within safe SAML spec limits. Deploying now.
EU users are confirming successful logins now. Incident resolved. Total impact: 39 minutes, ~200 users.

Prevention items: (1) Add NTP drift monitoring alert for all Okta cluster nodes via Datadog. (2) Add post-data-center-migration checklist item: validate NTP sync and SAML assertion timing before cutting over. (3) Document the clock skew config location for future on-call engineers.
Agreed. I'll create the Datadog monitor. Someone should write up the runbook and link it to the IT-4071 ticket. Marking this thread as resolved.
